item.title: Bank stocks under pressure after Australia targets property investors
item.description: Commonwealth Bank slides 10 per cent amid budget changes and darkening economic outlook

item.title: Miami got NYC’s billionaires. Will it get their businesses?
item.description: Wall Street South vs Wall Street North

item.title: Tech Exchange
item.description: This series features conversations between FT journalists and the technology industry’s leaders, innovators, and thinkers. In the latest instalment, Amazon’s Panos Panay, the head of the group’s device unit talks about the hardware push that follows Alexa+

item.title: Introducing: The Story of Money
item.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

item.title: BuzzFeed and GoPro learn nostalgia doesn’t pay the bills
item.description: The goodwill of middle-aged consumers won’t be enough to generate a meaningful return

item.title: The takeover that’s the talk of London
item.description: The £10.6bn showdown between EQT and Intertek is pitting top UK rainmakers against each other

item.title: UK veterinary group CVS targeted by activist investor
item.description: Converium Capital complains vet company’s stock has not recovered after UK regulators finished an industry probe

item.title: MUFG seeks to offload risk tied to $2bn of private credit loans
item.description: Japanese lender in talks over risk transfer as private capital industry is under strain

item.title: Don’t let bad thinking scupper your house sale
item.description: Price fixation locks in vendors when housing market falters

item.title: Nintendo needs Super Mario once again
item.description: The little plumber is a bulwark of intellectual property as rising chip prices pressure the company

item.title: Europe’s few AI plays soar as US tech frenzy goes global
item.description: Investors hunt for winners in market that for years has lagged behind Wall Street’s rally

item.title: Bezos’s Blue Origin weighs first external fundraising
item.description: Rocket maker’s chief tells staff it will need outside capital to meet ambitious launch plans

item.title: Inheritance tax plans for pensions could cause family disputes, experts warn
item.description: Schemes will be allowed to retain half of savings pot to cover potential tax bills

item.title: Private credit isn’t the problem — concentration is
item.description: Diversification is the foundation of good investing

item.title: The Iran war and the future of cheap flights
item.description: Low-cost air travel may be a thing of the past as rising fuel prices tip carriers into another crisis

item.title: Hormuz closure stalls construction projects as material costs soar
item.description: Building schemes around the world are being held up by shortages of products derived from oil

item.title: JPMorgan is finally rolling out its consumer bank in Europe
item.description: Almost five years after launching its digital bank Chase in the UK, CEO Jamie Dimon is preparing to take on Germany

item.title: Brazilian gunmaker Taurus eyes shift from civilian pistols to military contracts
item.description: Having ridden a surge in gun sales at home and in the US, Porto Alegre company is now targeting overseas defence contracts

item.title: Porto Alegre nurtures growing list of new technology ventures
item.description: City has ambitions to become regional centre for innovation, but is competing with more established rivals

item.title: Call for entries: UK’s Fastest-Growing Companies 2027
item.description: We are looking for organisations that have done most to increase revenues in England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland

item.title: Amazon’s Panos Panay: We’re not necessarily going after a phone
item.description: Executive tasked with driving ‘beauty’ and profitability at group’s device unit on hardware push that follows Alexa+

item.title: Why money is the biggest shared hallucination in human history
item.description: What human-sized stones on the island of Yap reveal about the true nature of money

item.title: AI Labs: Google DeepMind plans its comeback
item.description: Google and its AI lab DeepMind are bearing down OpenAI and Anthropic

item.title: JPMorgan set to reshuffle investment bank chiefs amid wider shake-up
item.description: Dorothee Blessing, Kevin Foley and Jared Kaye expected to become co-heads of global investment banking

item.title: Will Chinese companies still move to Singapore after Manus crackdown?
item.description: Beijing’s move to block takeover of AI start-up with headquarters in city-state challenges sustainability of ‘Singapore washing’

item.title: 1MDB financier Jho Low asks Trump for a pardon
item.description: Alleged mastermind behind multibillion-dollar scandal appears in US filing that lists pardon requests

item.title: Ad ban for lab gems is diamonds’ best friend
item.description: UK watchdog rules jewellers cannot use ‘diamond’ in isolation in synthetic stones’ adverts

item.title: FirstFT: China’s tech giants miss out on AI stock market rally
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: US Treasury secretary meets Japanese PM, and US inflation jumps to 3.8%

item.title: Resignation of US food and drug regulator head adds to mounting agency vacancies
item.description: Lawyer Kyle Diamantas appointed FDA’s acting leader as concerns mount over hantavirus outbreak

item.title: China’s big tech groups miss out on AI stock market frenzy
item.description: Tencent and Alibaba left behind as investors focus on pure AI plays
